mechanism_narrative: A persistently low or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate on equity cash flows and lifts risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive when policy is not tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.4% in 2016-10, indicating an extremely low-rate regime
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than unconditional baseline during low FEDFUNDS periods, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls to or below 5.0% after already being below 5.5%, labor slack is limited and equities price stronger wage pressure and tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.9 in 2016-10, near cycle-low levels
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll demand and stronger nominal retail sales, which should support cyclically sensitive earnings and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS and RSAFS were both elevated in the 2016-10 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-payroll-and-retail-sales condition does not outperform either series alone, or if the interaction term has a non-positive sign in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in INDPRO signals an industrial slowdown and future earnings downgrades; when monthly industrial production falls from the prior month, equity returns over the next week should be negative.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value in 2016-09 was above later revised value, showing the series can move materially
failure_condition: Reject if negative monthly INDPRO changes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears after controlling for unconditional drift.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth accelerates on a quarterly basis and HOUST is rising, the economy is in an expanding demand phase that supports earnings revisions and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:2016 snapshots show positive housing starts and ongoing GDP expansion
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-HOUST combination does not improve predictive power versus either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive in high-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above recent trend while FEDFUNDS remains near the lower bound implies rising inflation pressure without policy room, which should compress multiples and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate rises above its 12-month average by more than 0.5pp
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 241.002 on 2016-09 with FEDFUNDS at 0.4
regime_hint: inflation scare under low policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration episodes do not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect vanishes after stratifying by low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An uptick in PAYEMS combined with a stable or falling UNRATE indicates genuine employment creation rather than labor-force shrinkage, and this should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through better consumption and earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE does not rise more than 0.1pp over 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.9 while PAYEMS was near cycle highs in 2016-10
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS gains without rising UNRATE do not deliver positive excess 20-day returns, or if the combined signal is not stronger than PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in RSAFS following already weak housing starts signals cooling household demand and softer near-term revenue growth, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative in late-cycle slowdowns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth turns negative and HOUST is below its 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value in 2016-10 was above later revised value, showing demand data can soften materially
failure_condition: Reject if the combined weak-retail-and-weak-housing condition does not predict below-baseline 60-day returns, or if the signal's sign is positive in a majority of triggered episodes.
